LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITION'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. SEIU HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT AN INJUNCTION IS NECESSARY FOR AN EVENTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD TO BE EFFECTIVE NOR HAS IT SHOWN IRREPARABLE INJURY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL UNION'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 302 PER JUDGE BUSCH'S RECUSAL AND TO BE HEARD ON JANUARY 27, 2010. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL UNION'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITION DENIED. A SEVERE BUDGET SHORTFALL RESULTED IN THE LAYOFFS OF THE SUBJECT EMPLOYEES. THE LAYOFFS ARE AN EXCLUSIVE MANAGEMENT DECISION WHICH DOES NOT PROPERLY COME BEFORE AN ARBITRATOR. (SEE ENGINEERS & ARCHITECTS ASSN. VS. COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DEPT. (1994) 30 CAL.APP. 4TH 644.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local Union'S Petition To Compel Arbitration; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jan-27-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local Union's Petition To Compel Arbitration. Anne I. Yen, Attorney at Law, (510) 337-1001 appearing for Petitioner and Moving party; Vincent A. Harrington, Jr. Esq., of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld (510) 337-1001 also appearing for Moving party; Elizabeth Salveson, Esq. Chief Labor Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3809 appearing for CCSF. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Petition denied. A severe budget shortfall resulted in the layoffs of the subject employees. The layoffs are an exclusive management decision which does not properly come before an arbitrator. (See Engineers & Architects Assn. vs. Community Development Dept. (1994) 30 Cal.App. 4th 644.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local Union'S Petition To Compel Arbitration; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jan-11-2010 Continued To Jan-27-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
Petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local Union'S Petition To Compel Arbitration IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 3 0 2 PER JUDGE BUSCH'S RECUSAL AND TO BE HEARD ON JANUARY 27, 2010. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Notice Of Hearing And Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jan-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL UNION'S Petition To Compel Arbitration IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 11, 2010 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PHA

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Nov-25-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Petition's Order to show cause re: preliminary injunction. Appearances: Vincent A. Harrington, Jr. Esq., Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld (510) 337-1001 appearing for Petitioner and moving party; Elizabeth Salveson, Esq. Office of the City Atty. CCSF (415) 554-3809 appearing for Respondent and opposing party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for a preliminary injunction is denied. SEIU has not established that an injunction is necessary for an eventual arbitration award to be effective nor has it shown irreparable injury. Order signed in open Court.(302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797
